stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of hundreds of ceasefire violations
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says his nation's forces would respond "symmetrically" to Russian attacks.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz as Iran peace talks fail
stories.summary: Reopening the critical waterway is a sticking point in negotiations as oil supply bottlenecks prompt a global energy crisis

stories.headline: Trump Says US Will Blockade Hormuz Strait After Iran Talks Fail
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the US would blockade the Strait of Hormuz following the collapse of peace talks with Iran in Islamabad. Axios National Security Editor Dave Lawler, Bloomberg News White House Reporter Jeff Mason and Congressional Reporter Alicia Diaz join Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: After 21 Hours of Talks With Iran, Vance Leaves Without a Deal
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance landed in Islamabad on Saturday tasked with ending six weeks of war and overcoming 47 years of enmity — 21 hours later, he flew home without a deal.

stories.headline: Trump Was Watching a U.F.C. Fight in Miami While Iran Talks Collapsed
stories.summary: On his way to Florida, President Trump said it did not matter to him if a deal with Iran was reached or not: “We win, regardless,” he said.

stories.headline: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
stories.summary: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

stories.headline: Navy Warships Cross Strait of Hormuz to Clear Mines, U.S. Says
stories.summary: Iran denied that the American destroyers had entered the strait, as negotiations for an extended cease-fire continued in Islamabad.

stories.headline: Penny Wong calls failed peace talks between US and Iran ‘disappointing’ and urges resumption

stories.headline: Inside Vance’s Iran negotiations: No deal, but ‘friendly’ talks
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance’s negotiations in Pakistan with Iran failed to end the war, but progress was reported on building goodwill.

stories.headline: Trump orders Strait of Hormuz blockade after US-Iran talks fail in Islamabad
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump ordered a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz after talks with Iran in Islamabad ended without a deal. The move ramps up pressure as both sides blame each other, while Pakistan urges restraint and the ceasefire hangs in the balance.

stories.headline: 'A truly historic moment': BBC reports from Hungary as Viktor Orbán concedes election
stories.summary: Rajini Vaidyanathan broadcasts from outside Hungary's parliament as crowds hear about the prime minister's concession.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Orbán concedes defeat as opposition heads for landslide win
stories.summary: Tisza party led by Péter Magyar projected to win a clear majority in a crushing loss for EU’s longest-serving premier

stories.headline: Hormuz Tracker: Limited Transits Before Trump Announces Blockade
stories.summary: Shipping flows through the Strait of Hormuz continued to operate at reduced levels on Sunday, before President Donald Trump announced an immediate blockade of the critical maritime chokepoint by the US Navy.

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
stories.summary: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

stories.headline: Hungary election live: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in Hungarian election after 16 years in power

stories.headline: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat as opposition wins Hungarian election
stories.summary: Win for Tisza party ends rightwing populist’s 16-year grip on power and will likely reshape country’s relationship with EUHungary election liveHungary’s opposition Tisza party, led by Péter Magyar, has won the general election, ending leader Viktor Orbán’s 16-year grip on power, in a result that is likely to rattle the White House and reshape the country’s relationship with the EU.Less than three hours after polls closed on Sunday, Orbán conceded

stories.headline: Hungary’s Viktor Orban concedes defeat in parliamentary elections
stories.summary: Hungary’s Viktor Orban has conceded defeat to opposition leader Peter Magyar after 16 years as prime minister.

stories.headline: Peter Magyar’s Tisza wins Hungary election as Viktor Orban concedes
stories.summary: Longtime Prime Minister Viktor Orban has conceded defeat in the country's parliamentary election.

stories.headline: Trump says US to blockade ships crossing Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump says the US Navy will immediately blockade the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Orbán publicly concedes election defeat, says Fidesz will serve Hungary in opposition
stories.summary: Final results won’t be known for several hours yet and with voter turnout at a record 77.8%, the highest ever recorded in a Hungarian election, there are plenty of ballot papers to count.

stories.headline: Ukraine and Russia accuse each other of breaching 36-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire
stories.summary: According to Ukraine's General Staff of the armed forces, by 7am the ceasefire had been violated by Russia 2,299 times, including assaults, shelling and small drone launches. However, Russia has refrained from using long-range drones, missiles or guided bombs.

stories.headline: Civilians feared killed after reports of air strike on Nigerian market
stories.summary: The Nigerian Air Force has not addressed reports it struck a village market while targeting militants.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Eric Swalwell Sexual Misconduct Allegations
stories.summary: Accusations that Mr. Swalwell, a congressman running for governor in California, sexually assaulted a former staffer and behaved inappropriately with other women have upended the race.

stories.headline: Switzerland’s Zug becomes bolt-hole for Gulf-based wealth
stories.summary: Small Swiss town is welcoming individuals and companies seeking refuge from war in the Middle East

stories.headline: Earnings Season Kicks Off With War, AI Threat Among Key Worries
stories.summary: Earnings season is about to start at a crucial time for stocks traders whipsawed by war in the Middle East, concerns around private credit and the disruptive threat of artificial intelligence.

stories.headline: Oil-Rich Latin America Lures Traders Navigating War Jitters
stories.summary: Latin American assets have emerged as a haven for emerging-market investors trying to navigate an increasingly volatile global backdrop, buoyed by a roster of oil exporters and greater insulation from tensions in the Middle East.

stories.headline: Calls grow for Rep. Swalwell to resign or be expelled over misconduct claims
stories.summary: Several Democratic and Republican lawmakers said they would be in favor of voting to expel Swalwell, as well as Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas), who both face misconduct allegations.

stories.headline: Peru Extends Voting for President, Congress Amid Delays at Polling Sites
stories.summary: Peru’s nationwide elections suffered severe delays at some polling stations, especially in the capital Lima, muddying what was already a complex race among a record 36 presidential candidates.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 4/12/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Israel’s Channel 13 News Senior Journalist Neria Kraus, Market Intelligence Founder & Director Amrita Sen, “Rory” Author Alan Shipnuck, AlphaCoreWealth Advisory Chief Investment Strategist Dr. David Stubss, Former National Security Advisor Under Don

stories.headline: Norway Averts Strike as Unions Clinch Wage Deal With Employers
stories.summary: Norway’s largest trade unions reached a wage deal with employers for private-sector workers, preventing a strike in western Europe’s top energy exporter.

stories.headline: Nation's Top Banks Set to Release Earnings After Tough Quarter
stories.summary: The KBW Bank Index had its weakest quarterly performance since 2023, which is raising concerns about the banking sector's recent challenges ahead of the upcoming earnings report from Wall Street's six largest banks. Bloomberg Intelligence Senior Analyst Herman Chan and Bloomberg News Managing Editor Kristine Aquino provide insight on what investors might expect to Christina Ruffini and Tim Stenovec on Bloomberg This Weekend. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Event Bets Pose a Problem for Wall Street Firms Looking to Trade
stories.summary: Prediction markets are booming. Wagers on everything from the return of Jesus Christ to who will win the World Cup have driven weekly volumes on platforms like Polymarket and Kalshi into the billions of dollars.

stories.headline: Trump Fires Board That Oversees Presidio in San Francisco
stories.summary: All six board members that oversee the national historic landmark were terminated, part of the administration’s efforts to reshape the federal bureaucracy.

stories.headline: Polls close in Benin with finance minister expected to win election
stories.summary: Former President Patrice Talon has stepped down after two five-year terms.

stories.headline: Peru votes for ninth president in less than decade
stories.summary: Voters to choose from 35 presidential candidates, including a comedian, a media baron and a political dynasty heiress.

stories.headline: Voters cast ballots in pivotal Hungarian election
stories.summary: Hungarians could see opposition leader Peter Magyar oust Prime Minister Viktor Orban, who has ruled for 16 years

stories.headline: Explaining Benin’s presidential election
stories.summary: Benin is voting for its next president. Al Jazeera’s Ahmed Idris is in Cotonou, explaining what’s at stake.

EU’s largest economies vs top US states: How do they compare in GDP?
stories.summary: Top EU countries and US states appear one after the other in economic size rankings. However, US states surpass the top EU economies in GDP per capita, both in nominal terms and purchasing power.

stories.headline: Vance, who wasn’t keen on Iran war, now tasked with trying to end it
stories.summary: The weekend’s peace talks in Islamabad mark Vance’s highest-profile assignment as vice president, with concomitant political risk.

stories.headline: Who is Viktor Orbán, Hungarian PM fighting to stay in power after 16 years?

stories.headline: Palestinian shot dead during Israeli settler attack on occupied West Bank village
stories.summary: The killing came as ex-security chiefs warned "government sponsored Jewish terrorism" was out of control.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.summary: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
stories.summary: Legal action follows war of words with Sentebale chair after Duke of Sussex’s resignation as patronThe Duke of Sussex is being sued by Sentebale in the latest twist in the bitter fallout over the African charity he co-founded.The charity has lodged papers in London’s high court over defamation claims naming Prince Harry and the former Sentebale trustee Mark Dyer as defendants. stories.headline: Russia and Ukraine agree to Orthodox Easter truce
stories.summary: Vladimir Putin announced the truce, which will last from Saturday afternoon through Easter Sunday.

stories.headline: Eliot Engel, a prominent voice on foreign affairs in the House, dies at 79
stories.summary: The New York Democrat helped lead the first impeachment inquiry into President Donald Trump. He served for 16 terms before losing his reelection bid in 2020.

stories.headline: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi Jinping in Beijing
stories.summary: Cheng Li-wun is the Kuomintang's first sitting leader to visit China in a decade.

stories.headline: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.summary: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.headline: Al Jazeera condemns killing of journalist in Israeli strike in Gaza
stories.summary: The Israeli military claims Mohammed Wishah was a "Hamas terrorist" - which the Qatar-based network has previously denied.

stories.headline: He's Australia's most decorated soldier. Now he's at the centre of a historic war crimes case
stories.summary: Ben Roberts-Smith's case is not only unprecedented for Australia but "extraordinary" for the globe too, scholars say.

stories.headline: French far-right leader romantically linked to Italian princess
stories.summary: Presidential hopeful Jordan Bardella was pictured on a Corsican beach with Princess Maria Carolina of Bourbon-Two Sicilies.

stories.headline: Argentina passes bill loosening protection of its glaciers
stories.summary: The new bill will make it easier for provincial governments to allow mining projects in glacier regions.

stories.headline: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost

stories.summary: The centuries-old Fanti Carnival returned to Nigeria on Monday with a burst of colour and spectacle.

stories.headline: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing's ambition
stories.summary: The AI agent sparked a frenzy of "raising lobsters" in March, with users training the tool to suit their needs.
